description: A Balwyn rear garden transformed from exotic lawn to a structured native planting. Indigenous species now dominate across 120m2. Habitat logs, rock refuges and mycorrhizal planting establish the ecological foundation from day one.

habitat.planting_method: Layered ecological planting - stepping-stone habitat zones

fauna_sightings.notes: Two skinks observed in rock refuge zone shortly after installation.

activity_log.title: Baseline assessment -- connected habitat landscape
activity_log.notes: Front and rear gardens documented as one connected system. 28 indigenous species recorded across groundcover, grass, shrub and small tree layers. Strong pollinator succession through banksia, correa, hardenbergia and kangaroo paw. Proximity to Maranoa Gardens noted as significant connectivity asset.

activity_log.title: Installation begins -- soil, mulch, habitat rocks and micro-topology
activity_log.notes: Installation commenced today. Underperforming vegetation removed including camellia and buxus. New soil and mulch installed. Habitat rocks placed throughout the garden beds. Micro-topology created across planting zones to guide water movement and create varied conditions for plant establishment. Rocks selected for both aesthetic presence and ecological function -- thermal mass, shelter and soil anchoring.

activity_log.title: Two skinks observed -- habitat rock zone
activity_log.notes: Two skinks observed in the rock refuge zone shortly after installation. Immediate fauna response to habitat rocks confirms thermal mass and shelter function. Adds to corridor node weighting.

activity_log.title: Planting, mycorrhiza inoculant, mulch and irrigation
activity_log.notes: Full planting installation completed. All 31 documented species planted with mycorrhiza inoculant applied at root zone. Finishing mulch layer installed. Drip irrigation system connected and tested. All plants hand watered in.

activity_log.title: Handmade Habitats -- natural material habitat structures installed
activity_log.notes: Habitat elements from Handmade Habitats -- hollowed logs set half into the ground, packed with sticks, dried grasses, gum nuts, native seeds, dried eucalyptus leaves and paper daisies. Designed to attract native bees, beetles, lizards and invertebrates. The embedded base creates a moist microclimate at soil level. Paper daisies closed in the rain on installation day. Local craft production from natural materials.

notes: Values reflect day-of-planting installation. Structural layers, canopy and soil health will be reassessed at 6 and 12 months as planting establishes.
